summary: mongoose-schema-unique executes remote code from jsonkeeper.com pastebin on require()
details: On module load, index.js runs a top-level async IIFE that fetches JSON from https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/XVHGD (an anonymous, mutable pastebin) and posts the response's `data.data` field to a worker thread. worker.js receives the payload and executes it via `new Function("require", msg.payload)(require)`, granting the pastebin content full Node.js `require` access in the importing process. Any project that installs and requires this package gives the pastebin controller arbitrary code execution — full access to environment variables, filesystem, credentials, and outbound network from the developer or CI machine. The package name, description, and CHANGELOG mimic the legitimate `mongoose-unique-validator` package, so developers who mistype the dependency name will silently trigger the RCE channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
